disclaimer: This dashboard is a decision-support tool based on open-source intelligence (OSINT). It is NOT official guidance. Consult your embassy and local authorities for evacuation decisions.

sections.title: Air Defense / Interceptor Effectiveness
sections.summary: Day 15: MOD reported 9 ballistic + 33 drones 'engaged' — no intercept breakdown (Day 5 of opacity). Zero casualties on Day 15. However, Fujairah port oil facility fire from intercepted drone debris (Mar 14) — defense working but debris still causing damage. Iran's port evacuation warning raises stakes.
sections.analysis: Defense continues to hold — zero new deaths for 4 days despite continued attacks. But Fujairah debris fire at oil facility shows even successful intercepts create ground-level risk. MOD opacity on intercept rates entering second week.

sections.title: Attack Volume on UAE
sections.summary: Volume remains low (~38/day avg, Days 10-15) but Iran just threatened 3 UAE ports by name and Fujairah already hit by debris fire. Low volume ≠ low risk. Iran claims US launched Kharg strikes from UAE territory (RAK + 'near Dubai'), using that as justification for port targeting. KSA attack volume trending UP while UAE trends down — possible reallocation, not exhaustion.
sections.analysis: UPGRADED TO AMBER. Volume collapse is real but Iran's explicit naming of 3 UAE ports as targets is a qualitative escalation that changes the risk profile. The low-volume 'nuisance phase' could end abruptly if Iran retaliates for Kharg Island. Araghchi's claim that US attacked from UAE territory provides Iran a narrative justification for escalating against UAE directly.

sections.title: Airport & Airspace Operations
sections.summary: Emirates operating reduced schedule to 110+ destinations. Mar 14: 143 Emirates + 56 Flydubai flights. Mar 15: 156 Emirates + 64 Flydubai planned. Reduced operations extended through Mar 28. Air Canada canceled ALL Dubai flights until May. Oman Air regional cancellations through Mar 22. Air India running 36+ ad-hoc repatriation flights. DXB operational but not normal.
sections.analysis: Gradual improvement — Emirates up from near-zero to 156 flights/day. But reduced ops extended through Mar 28 signals airlines don't expect normalization soon. Air Canada's May cancellation is a market signal about expected conflict duration.

sections.title: Ports & Cargo Continuity
sections.summary: CRITICAL: Iran issued evacuation warning for Jebel Ali, Khalifa, and Fujairah ports (Mar 14). Claims US attacked Kharg Island from UAE territory. Fujairah oil facility fire from drone debris confirmed (AP). Three alternative supply routes active (Fujairah/Khorfakkan reroute, Oman Green Corridor), but Iran now explicitly targeting ports. Hormuz still 90% shut but cracks appearing — Iran allowed 2 Indian LPG carriers + 1 Saudi tanker through. Greek shipowners braving passage for profit.
sections.analysis: UPGRADED TO RED. Iran naming 3 UAE ports as targets is a first — previously claimed to only target US assets. Araghchi explicitly said US attacked from RAK and 'near Dubai,' providing Iran narrative cover for direct port strikes. Fujairah fire (drone debris) already demonstrates the risk. The alternative supply routes via Oman remain critical. Hormuz showing first cracks — selective transit being allowed for India/Turkey, but no systematic reopening. France + allies planning naval escort convoy system.

sections.title: Retail Prices & Consumer Stress
sections.summary: Government maintains supplies are adequate. Bloomberg confirmed shelves stocked. Price gouging enforcement active (4,468 inspections, 449 warnings). Three alternative supply routes operational (Fujairah, Khorfakkan, Oman Green Corridor). UAE imports 90% of food but has systematically built Hormuz bypass infrastructure. Fresh food supply chains under strain but functioning.
sections.analysis: Two weeks in, no mass shortages. Government response has been strong — enforcement, alternative routes, and consumer messaging all coordinated. The Oman Green Corridor specifically addresses food import vulnerability. Monitor fresh produce availability week-to-week but current evidence does not support shortage panic.

sections.title: Oman Border & Muscat Viability
sections.summary: Oman remains primary evacuation hub. NYT confirmed 600+ evacuated by bus, 1hr border queue. Oman Air Sharjah→Muscat bus link active. BA/QR/Emirates/Etihad joint evacuation via Muscat. Oman barely targeted (8 projectiles total). Border operational, route validated. Oman Air regional cancellations extended through Mar 22 limits outbound flight options from Muscat.
sections.analysis: Oman route remains viable and is the primary evacuation corridor. No credible reports of border closure or major delays beyond the ~1hr queue NYT documented on Mar 9. Oman Air cancellations limit flight options from Muscat but other carriers operating. Route: Dubai → Al Ain → Buraimi/Hili border → Muscat.

sections.title: Civilian & Infrastructure Escalation
sections.summary: CRITICAL ESCALATION: Iran issued evacuation warning for 3 UAE ports — first time openly threatening non-US assets. Araghchi claims US attacked Kharg from UAE territory ('RAK and near Dubai'). Fujairah port oil facility fire from drone debris (AP confirmed). Iran says it will 'try to be careful not to attack populated areas' — hedged language, not a guarantee. Trump threatens to 'wipe out' Kharg oil infrastructure, which Iran says would trigger 'new level of retaliation.'
sections.analysis: THIS IS THE BIGGEST ESCALATION SINCE DAY 1. Iran previously maintained the fiction that it was only targeting US assets in GCC states. Naming 3 UAE ports and issuing civilian evacuation warnings crosses a new line. Araghchi's claim that the US attacked from UAE territory gives Iran narrative justification for treating UAE infrastructure as legitimate military targets. The hedged language ('try to be careful') is NOT reassuring — it's diplomatic cover for potential strikes. Combined with Trump threatening Kharg oil infrastructure (which Iran says triggers 'new level of retaliation'), the escalation spiral is re-accelerating even as attack volumes stay low.

sections.title: Market-Implied Conflict Duration
sections.summary: Oil surging despite historic intervention. Brent $103, WTI $99 (up 17% SINCE IEA's 400M barrel release). IEA release = largest in 50-year history but 'nowhere near enough' per market. US releasing 172M barrels over 120 days (1.4M bpd = only 15% of lost Hormuz supply). Gold retreated to $5,032 from $5,420 record. Trump eased Russian oil sanctions to mitigate prices. Rystad: 2-month war → $110 Brent; 4-month war → $135 Brent by June.
sections.analysis: Market is saying the war is NOT ending soon. If the IEA's largest-ever stockpile release can't prevent oil from surging 17%, the market is pricing in a prolonged Hormuz closure. The SPR math is damning: 1.4M bpd release vs 9M bpd lost = covers only 15%. Russia sanctions easing is a desperation move. Gold retreating from $5,420 to $5,032 suggests some war premium unwinding, but oil tells the real story.

sections.title: Conflict Endgame — Is Tehran Still Coherent?
sections.summary: CEASEFIRE DEAD FOR NOW. Reuters exclusive: Trump rejected allied mediation efforts. NBC: Trump says 'Iran wants a deal, terms aren't good enough yet.' Demands complete nuclear abandonment. Iran FM: rejected ceasefire 'until US stops.' Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement (written only, never appeared in person): Hormuz stays closed, keep fighting. Trump questions whether Khamenei is 'even alive.' US struck Kharg Island military targets, threatens oil infrastructure next. 2,500 Marines + USS Tripoli deploying. Israel hit 200+ targets in 24hrs. UN Security Council resolution demands Iran stop GCC attacks.

metrics.label: Kharg Island
metrics.value: Military targets struck, oil spared (for now)

sections.analysis: The endgame just got further away. Both sides have explicitly rejected ceasefire. Trump wants complete nuclear surrender. Iran says no talks while bombs fall. Mojtaba Khamenei's written-only statement (never appeared in person — Trump questions if he's alive) vowed to keep Hormuz closed. The Kharg Island strike is the biggest single escalation of the war: US put a marker on Iran's economic jugular without cutting it, essentially saying 'we can kill your oil revenue whenever we want.' If Trump follows through on the oil infrastructure threat, Iran's parliament speaker warned of 'a new level of retaliation.' Both sides are escalating, not de-escalating. The war is not ending in the next week.

red.note: Day 11: Drone intercept at 74.3% — well below 85% threshold. 9 hit UAE territory.

red.trigger: Cruise missile wave targeting UAE cities
red.note: Day 12: 7 cruise missiles in single day. Cumulative now 15 (was 8 for first 11 days).
Bahrain desal hit — UAE not yet
3 drone/debris incidents near DXB. Latest Mar 11: 2 drones, 4 injured. Ops continue.

amber.note: No confirmed deliberate targeting — all residential damage is interception debris
Resumed limited schedule Mar 9 after Mar 7 suspension

amber.trigger: Second UAE fatality from direct hit
Iran deploys cruise missiles at Gulf cities

green.note: Trump: 'war very complete' — first US de-escalation signal. No formal ceasefire yet.
